concepts.finding: NCF: neural framework for collaborative filtering replacing dot product with neural architecture. Generalized MF (GMF): element-wise product of user/item embeddings. MLP: concatenation into fully connected layers. NeuMF: fused GMF + MLP with pretraining. Outperforms MF by 10-15% on top-K recommendation. MLP captures nonlinear interactions.
concepts.implication: Replace keyword-intent dot product with neural interaction function. GMF = element-wise product of keyword and intent embeddings. MLP = concatenation + DNN. NeuMF = both combined. Nonlinear interactions capture complex keyword-intent relationships that dot product misses.
concepts.open_question: Is a simple dot product sufficient for keyword-intent matching, or does nonlinear interaction (NCF/MLP) provide meaningful improvement?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Neural collaborative filtering (NeuMF) for keyword-intent scoring captures nonlinear patterns that improve recall@10 by 5%+ over dot product scoring, especially for multi-intent keywords.
